name: Panel
summary: Bordered, padded surface card for grouping related content into a visually distinct section; supports `normalizeRowHeights` for equal-height children — use it to frame a block, and `Box` for an unstyled container.

props.name: intent
props.description: Semantic color intent; maps to theme tokens

props.name: color
props.description: Explicit color override (theme token name or CSS color)

props.name: centerContent
props.description: Centre contents & propagate intent via CSS var

props.name: alignX
props.description: Horizontal placement of the panel when not fullWidth

props.name: normalizeRowHeights
props.description:
Opt out of row height normalization (when a parent Grid enables it).
Defaults to true (normalize). Set to false to keep intrinsic heights.

props.name: preset
props.description: One or many style-preset names registered via `definePreset()`

props.name: compact
props.description:
Zeros all layout spacing (pad, gap, and spacing-margins) where supported and
cascades to spacing-aware descendants. Does not touch control insets,
structural geometry, border-radius, glyph sizes, or alignment. `compact={false}`
opts a subtree back out of an inherited compact.

props.name: pad
props.description: Container padding as units or CSS length.

props.name: density
props.description: Density override for the spacing scale; defaults from nearest Surface.

bestPractices: Use Panel to group related content and provide visual separation.
Choose variant='outlined' when you want a transparent surface with an outline.
Prefer theme tokens for backgrounds; if you set a custom background ensure text maintains contrast.
Use presets to standardize repeating styles; avoid duplicating sx across pages.
Reserve fullWidth for sections that should span the container; otherwise, anchor with alignX.
Control row-height normalization with `normalizeRowHeights` (plural). Inside a Grid that normalizes row heights, set `normalizeRowHeights={false}` to keep intrinsic heights.
Center content with centerContent for compact UI elements, not long-form text blocks.
Nest Panels judiciously to create hierarchy; keep padding consistent using spacing tokens.

examples.title: Outlined on backgroundAlt surface
examples.code: <Box background={theme.colors['backgroundAlt']} sx={{ padding: theme.spacing(1) }}><Panel variant="outlined" sx={{ padding: theme.spacing(1) }}><Typography>Outline contrasts backgroundAlt</Typography></Panel></Box>

examples.title: Background + padding
examples.code: <Panel color={theme.colors['secondary']} sx={{ padding: theme.spacing(1) }}><Typography>Readable on themed background</Typography></Panel>
